grounding_note: Row 196 (stated misdemeanors but assault/strangulation conduct) split humans between Misdemeanor and Lesser Felony; the consensus rule keeps only one severity label. The disambiguating fact is what the charging document actually says — a fact the defendant holds (citation, indictment, release papers) and that narrative descriptions of the conduct systematically obscure.

scenarios.opening_query: I got arrested after a fight outside a bar that I didn't start. I need a criminal defense lawyer.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The paperwork says Assault III because the other guy's jaw was broken, and they told me that's a felony.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The charging papers say Assault III — they told me that's a felony because his jaw broke.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what specific charge appears on the citation or charging document
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The citation says Assault IV and Disorderly Conduct, both misdemeanors.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The citation lists Assault IV and disorderly conduct — both misdemeanors.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same brawl narrative supports either severity; only the charging document resolves it (row 196's exact ambiguity).

scenarios.opening_query: The police charged me with assault after an argument with my brother-in-law got physical. I'm terrified.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The complaint says Assault IV, a Class A misdemeanor; nobody was seriously hurt.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's Assault IV on the complaint — a Class A misdemeanor. Nobody was really hurt.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the degree of assault actually charged
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They charged Assault III as a felony because he needed stitches and I supposedly used a bottle.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's felony Assault III — they say I used a bottle and he needed stitches.
scenarios.vetting_note: Frightened tone pulls readers toward the severe reading; the complaint's degree is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm being charged with theft from my old job and I have a court date in three weeks.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The charge is Theft I as a felony because they claim the total was over $1,000.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's Theft I, a felony — they say the total was over a thousand dollars.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the theft degree charged and alleged dollar amount
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The charge is Theft III, a misdemeanor, over about $80 of merchandise.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's Theft III, a misdemeanor — about $80 of stuff they say I took.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dollar thresholds silently set severity in theft cases; users describe the accusation, not the statute.

scenarios.opening_query: My son was arrested over a fight at a party. We need a lawyer before his arraignment Friday.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DA charged Robbery I because they say he took the other kid's phone during the fight — that's a Measure 11 charge here.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They charged Robbery I — a Measure 11 offense — because he allegedly took the other kid's phone during the fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the exact charge filed and whether it carries mandatory minimums
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's charged with misdemeanor Harassment and nothing else.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just misdemeanor harassment on the paperwork.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Fight at a party' spans the full severity range; Measure 11 exposure changes everything, mirroring the taxonomy's Major Felony description.

scenarios.opening_query: I was arrested for domestic violence charges I dispute and I can't go home right now.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The release papers show one count of misdemeanor Assault IV constituting domestic violence.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The release paperwork shows one count — misdemeanor Assault IV, domestic violence.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what count and degree the release or charging papers show
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They charged felony Strangulation along with the assault count.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: There's a felony strangulation count on top of the assault.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 196 named strangulation as the severity-driving allegation; charging papers are the neutral disambiguator. Defense-side intake, handled factually.

scenarios.opening_query: Got a call that there's a warrant for me over a check I wrote to a contractor that bounced.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The warrant is for felony Negotiating a Bad Check because the amount was $4,700.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The warrant says felony bad check — the check was $4,700.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the charged offense level and check amount
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The warrant is for a misdemeanor over a $180 check.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a misdemeanor warrant — the check was $180.
scenarios.vetting_note: Amount-driven severity; the user knows the check amount but the opening omits it.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a defense attorney. I was arrested for drugs when police searched my friend's car.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: They charged me with delivery of a controlled substance because of how it was packaged, not just possession.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's charged as delivery, not possession — they say the packaging shows intent to sell.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the charge is possession or manufacturing/delivery
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a misdemeanor possession charge for a small personal amount.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just misdemeanor possession — a small personal amount.
scenarios.vetting_note: Possession-vs-delivery is the highest-stakes severity boundary in drug cases; taxonomy lists delivery under Major Felony.

scenarios.opening_query: Charged after a road rage incident where the other driver claims I threatened him. It's my word against his.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The actual charge filed is Menacing, a misdemeanor; there was no weapon involved.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The charge is Menacing, a misdemeanor — no weapon was involved.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the filed charge and whether a weapon allegation raises the level
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They filed felony Unlawful Use of a Weapon because he claims I waved a tire iron.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's felony unlawful use of a weapon — he says I waved a tire iron.
scenarios.vetting_note: Weapon allegation is the single fact separating the two routes in threat cases.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter got caught up in a shoplifting incident with her friends at the mall. What do we do?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Juvenile Offender || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's 19, so this is adult court — the citation says Theft III, a misdemeanor.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's 19, so it's adult court — the citation says Theft III, a misdemeanor.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the accused is a minor in juvenile court or an adult
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's 15, and the case is going through juvenile court.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's 15 — it's in juvenile court.
scenarios.vetting_note: Parent intake about a 'daughter' hides whether juvenile or adult jurisdiction applies; age is a real disclosure, not a role reversal.

scenarios.opening_query: I was cited after a hunting trip went wrong and a game officer stopped us on the way out.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Game Violations || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: Because it's my third violation they charged it as a felony and seized my rifle.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my third violation, so they filed it as a felony and took my rifle.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the citation is a wildlife violation or elevated to a felony
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a standard citation for exceeding the bag limit, a violation-level offense.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a regular over-the-bag-limit citation.
scenarios.vetting_note: Repeat-offense enhancement is a fact the user holds; opening reads as a routine game violation.

scenarios.opening_query: Police arrested my husband after a confrontation with a neighbor over our property line got out of hand.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The only charge on the citation is Criminal Trespass II, a misdemeanor.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The citation shows just Criminal Trespass II — a misdemeanor.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what the citation actually charges
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's charged with felony Criminal Mischief I because the neighbor's camera system was destroyed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's felony Criminal Mischief I — the neighbor's camera system got destroyed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Criminal Mischief I is the taxonomy's own Lesser Felony example; the damage fact sets severity.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm facing charges from a protest arrest downtown last month and my court date is coming up.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: They upgraded mine to felony Riot because I was allegedly near the group that broke windows.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Mine got upgraded to felony riot — they say I was near the group that broke windows.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the protest charge is disorderly conduct or an upgraded felony count
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The charge is Disorderly Conduct II, a misdemeanor, for blocking the street.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's Disorderly Conduct II for blocking the street — a misdemeanor.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same protest event, different filed count; the upgrade fact is decisive and user-held.

scenarios.opening_query: Charged after a bar tab dispute where the manager says I pushed him. Never been in trouble before.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: Because he hit his head on the counter, the DA filed it as felony Assault III.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He hit his head on the counter going down, so the DA filed felony Assault III.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the degree of assault filed given the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The charge is misdemeanor Harassment for offensive physical contact; he wasn't hurt.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's misdemeanor harassment — offensive contact, no injury.
scenarios.vetting_note: Injury outcome, not the push itself, sets the charge level; user narrates the push.

scenarios.opening_query: My roommate accused me of stealing her jewelry and now I have a criminal case against me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The complaint charges Theft II as a misdemeanor, valuing the items at about $300.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The complaint says Theft II, a misdemeanor — they valued it around $300.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the theft degree and valuation charged
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They charged felony Theft I because she claims the ring alone is worth $2,500.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's felony Theft I — she says the ring alone is worth $2,500.
scenarios.vetting_note: Valuation disputes between the parties directly set the felony/misdemeanor line.

scenarios.opening_query: I need help with a criminal charge from an incident at my storage unit involving stuff that wasn't mine.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The indictment charges first-degree Burglary because they say I entered the neighboring occupied caretaker unit.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The indictment says Burglary I — they claim I went into the occupied caretaker's unit next door.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what the indictment charges and whether an occupied dwelling is alleged
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a misdemeanor second-degree criminal trespass citation for being in the facility after hours.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just a trespass misdemeanor for being there after hours.
scenarios.vetting_note: Occupied-dwelling element separates Measure 11 Burglary I from simple trespass; vague opening is realistic.

scenarios.opening_query: There's a criminal case against me from a fight at my kid's football game. Other parent pressed charges.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: Because the other parent is 68, they filed it as felony assault on a vulnerable person.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The other parent is 68, so they filed it as a felony under the vulnerable-person law.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a victim characteristic elevates the charged offense
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's charged as Assault IV, a misdemeanor; we're both in our thirties and he wasn't injured badly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's Assault IV, a misdemeanor — we're both in our thirties.
scenarios.vetting_note: Victim-age enhancement is a statute fact users don't realize matters until asked.

scenarios.opening_query: I was charged over an incident at work where money went missing from the till.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor
scenarios.hidden_fact: The charge is misdemeanor Theft III; the till was short $95.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's misdemeanor Theft III — the till was short $95.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the amount alleged and degree charged
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They charged felony Aggravated Theft because they're claiming a pattern totaling $12,000 over a year.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's felony aggravated theft — they claim it adds up to $12,000 over a year.
scenarios.vetting_note: Aggregation of small takings into one felony count is a charging fact only the paperwork reveals.

scenarios.opening_query: Police showed up at my door and arrested me over something my ex told them happened months ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony || Criminal Law > Major Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: The charges include first-degree Kidnapping, a Measure 11 offense, because she says I wouldn't let her leave the apartment that night.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They charged Kidnapping I — Measure 11 — because she says I wouldn't let her leave that night.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the specific counts charged and whether any carry mandatory minimums
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The only charge is misdemeanor Telephonic Harassment over some calls after we broke up.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's one misdemeanor count — telephonic harassment over calls after the breakup.
scenarios.vetting_note: Delayed-report DV-adjacent case spanning the widest severity range; charge sheet is the fact. Kept factual and defense-neutral.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother got arrested driving home last weekend and we found out there are charges beyond the traffic stop.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Lesser Felony
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's charged with felony Attempt to Elude because he didn't pull over for two miles.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's felony attempt to elude — he didn't stop for about two miles.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what charges beyond the traffic violation were filed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It turned out to be just the speeding ticket plus an expired registration violation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: In the end it's just the speeding ticket and expired tags.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Beyond the traffic stop' invites the clarifying question; elude is the severity fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I have court next month for charges from a misunderstanding at a self-checkout machine.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Misdemeanor || Criminal Law > Local Ordinances
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's actually a city ordinance violation ticket, not a state criminal charge — the store and city handle it that way for first-timers.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It turned out to be a city ordinance ticket, not a state charge — first-time policy.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the citation is a state criminal charge or a municipal ordinance violation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The citation is state-court misdemeanor Theft III.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a state misdemeanor — Theft III.
scenarios.vetting_note: Municipal-vs-state forum is a genuine routing fact for petty theft; distinct exact labels in the taxonomy.
